area_description: Speech and Audio LLMs encompass techniques for understanding, generating, and processing speech and audio signals using large language models, spanning speech recognition, synthesis, speech-text joint modeling, audio understanding, translation, and signal enhancement.

categories.name: Speech Understanding
categories.description: Methods for extracting linguistic and paralinguistic information from speech signals, including transcription, intent recognition, speaker characteristics, and emotional state detection.

sub_topics.name: Automatic Speech Recognition
sub_topics.description: Converting spoken language into text, including end-to-end ASR models, attention-based encoder-decoders, and CTC-based approaches.

sub_topics.name: Spoken Language Understanding
sub_topics.description: Extracting semantic meaning, intent, and structured information directly from speech signals, including slot filling and intent classification from audio.

sub_topics.name: Speaker Identification
sub_topics.description: Recognizing and verifying speaker identity from speech, including speaker diarization, verification, and embedding extraction.

sub_topics.name: Emotion Recognition
sub_topics.description: Detecting emotional states and sentiment from speech signals, leveraging prosodic, spectral, and linguistic cues.

categories.name: Speech Generation
categories.description: Techniques for synthesizing speech from text or other conditioning signals, including neural TTS, voice cloning, controllable synthesis, and prosody modeling.

sub_topics.name: Text-to-Speech Synthesis
sub_topics.description: Converting text into natural-sounding speech using neural network architectures including autoregressive, non-autoregressive, and diffusion-based models.

sub_topics.name: Voice Cloning
sub_topics.description: Replicating a target speaker's voice characteristics from limited reference audio, enabling zero-shot and few-shot speaker adaptation.

sub_topics.name: Speech Synthesis Control
sub_topics.description: Controlling attributes of generated speech such as style, emotion, speaking rate, and emphasis through conditioning mechanisms.

sub_topics.name: Prosody Modeling
sub_topics.description: Modeling and generating natural prosodic patterns including intonation, rhythm, stress, and phrasing in synthesized speech.

categories.name: Speech-Language Models
categories.description: Architectures and methods that integrate speech and language modalities within large language models, including speech tokenization strategies, joint speech-text training, and spoken dialogue systems.

sub_topics.name: Speech Tokenization
sub_topics.description: Converting continuous speech signals into discrete token sequences suitable for language model processing, including neural codec models and self-supervised speech tokens.

sub_topics.name: Speech-Text Joint Models
sub_topics.description: Models trained jointly on speech and text data to learn shared representations and enable cross-modal transfer between modalities.

sub_topics.name: Audio LLMs
sub_topics.description: Large language models extended to process and generate audio inputs and outputs, enabling instruction-following and general-purpose audio understanding.

sub_topics.name: Spoken Dialogue Models
sub_topics.description: Models designed for real-time spoken conversation, supporting turn-taking, backchanneling, and full-duplex speech-to-speech interaction.

categories.name: Audio Understanding
categories.description: Methods for analyzing and interpreting non-speech audio signals, including environmental sounds, music, and general audio events, often leveraging large-scale pre-training.

sub_topics.name: Audio Captioning
sub_topics.description: Generating natural language descriptions of audio content, bridging the gap between audio perception and language generation.

sub_topics.name: Sound Event Detection
sub_topics.description: Detecting, classifying, and localizing sound events within audio streams, including polyphonic event detection and audio tagging.

sub_topics.name: Music Understanding
sub_topics.description: Analyzing musical content including genre classification, instrument recognition, music transcription, and music-language models.

sub_topics.name: Environmental Audio Analysis
sub_topics.description: Understanding and classifying ambient and environmental sounds, including acoustic scene classification and audio scene understanding.

categories.name: Speech Translation
categories.description: Techniques for translating speech from one language to another, including end-to-end approaches, cascaded pipelines, simultaneous translation, and handling of code-switching scenarios.

sub_topics.name: End-to-End Speech Translation
sub_topics.description: Directly translating speech in one language to text or speech in another language without intermediate ASR output, using single unified models.

sub_topics.name: Cascaded Translation Systems
sub_topics.description: Multi-stage speech translation pipelines combining ASR and machine translation components, with techniques to mitigate error propagation.

sub_topics.name: Simultaneous Speech Translation
sub_topics.description: Translating speech in real-time as it is being spoken, balancing translation quality with latency constraints.

sub_topics.name: Code-Switching in Speech
sub_topics.description: Handling speech that alternates between two or more languages within a single utterance, including recognition and translation of code-mixed speech.

categories.name: Speech Enhancement and Processing
categories.description: Signal processing and deep learning techniques for improving speech quality, including separating mixed audio sources, reducing noise, extending bandwidth, and converting voice characteristics.

sub_topics.name: Speech Separation
sub_topics.description: Separating individual speech sources from mixed audio signals, including the cocktail party problem and multi-talker scenarios.

sub_topics.name: Noise Reduction
sub_topics.description: Removing background noise and interference from speech signals using deep learning-based and traditional signal processing methods.

sub_topics.name: Bandwidth Extension
sub_topics.description: Extending the frequency bandwidth of narrowband speech to wideband or super-wideband, improving perceived audio quality.

sub_topics.name: Voice Conversion
sub_topics.description: Transforming speech from one speaker's voice to sound like another speaker while preserving linguistic content.

theme.name: Multilingual Speech
theme.description: Papers addressing speech processing across multiple languages, including multilingual pre-training, cross-lingual transfer, and polyglot models.

theme.name: Low-resource Languages
theme.description: Papers focusing on speech technologies for under-resourced and endangered languages with limited training data.

theme.name: Real-time Processing
theme.description: Papers addressing latency constraints and streaming architectures for real-time speech and audio processing.

theme.name: Expressive Speech
theme.description: Papers on generating and understanding expressive speech with rich emotional, stylistic, and paralinguistic variations.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply the techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
